meta.certificationName: AWS Certified Generative AI Developer – Professional
meta.title: AWS-GAP-3: Bedrock Design, Evaluation, Safety and Operations

questions.stem: A team is building a chat application on Amazon Bedrock and wants to maintain a multi-turn conversation with a foundation model using a chat-oriented request structure. Which API should they use first?

options.explanation: The Converse API is designed for conversational inference patterns. The other APIs serve agents, knowledge bases, or monitoring.

questions.reference: [Using the Converse API (moved) - Amazon BedrockUsing the Converse API (moved) - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/conversation-inference-call.html)

questions.stem: A workload needs straightforward single-request inference against a model without agent orchestration. Which Amazon Bedrock API is most appropriate?

options.explanation: InvokeModel is the direct inference API for a model request. The other operations manage resources or evaluations rather than basic inference.

questions.reference: [Submit a single prompt with InvokeModel (moved) - Amazon BedrockSubmit a single prompt with InvokeModel (moved) - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/inference-invoke.html)

questions.stem: When selecting a foundation model for a new application, what should the engineer compare first?

options.text: Model capabilities and supported features against the workload requirements
options.explanation: Model selection should be based on capabilities, modalities, context, latency, and other workload-relevant features.

options.text: Only the alphabetical order of model names
Only whether the model vendor has a logo

questions.reference: [Model support by feature in Amazon Bedrock - Amazon BedrockModel support by feature in Amazon Bedrock - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/models-features.html)

questions.stem: A product team needs grounded answers over internal documents rather than relying only on the model's pretrained knowledge. Which design pattern is most appropriate?

options.text: Retrieval-augmented generation with a knowledge base
options.explanation: Knowledge-base-backed retrieval grounds responses on enterprise content. Monitoring and storage policies do not add retrieval context.

questions.reference: [Retrieve data and generate AI responses with Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases - Amazon BedrockRetrieve data and generate AI responses with Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/knowledge-base.html)

questions.stem: A Bedrock application must route model calls through a consistent configuration that can span Regions and model backends. Which Bedrock feature is relevant?

options.explanation: Inference profiles help standardize inference routing/configuration patterns. The other services are not Bedrock inference routing features.

questions.reference: [Set up a model invocation resource using inference profiles - Amazon BedrockSet up a model invocation resource using inference profiles - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/inference-profiles.html)

questions.stem: A developer wants the application to call business APIs as part of a generated workflow rather than only returning text. Which Bedrock capability best fits?

options.explanation: Agents orchestrate model reasoning and action execution against connected systems. Guardrails and evaluation do not perform API actions.

questions.reference: [Automate tasks in your application using AI agents - Amazon BedrockAutomate tasks in your application using AI agents - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/agents.html)

questions.stem: Within an Amazon Bedrock agent, what is the primary purpose of an action group?

options.text: It defines callable operations the agent can use to fulfill tasks
options.explanation: Action groups expose operations and schemas that an agent can invoke. They do not replace IAM or serve as monitoring storage.

options.text: It encrypts all prompts at rest

questions.reference: [Add an action group to your agent in Amazon Bedrock - Amazon BedrockAdd an action group to your agent in Amazon Bedrock - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/agents-action-add.html)

questions.stem: A team wants to standardize reusable prompts, versions, and deployment across multiple applications. Which Bedrock feature should they adopt?

options.explanation: Prompt management is intended for reusable managed prompts and versions. The networking features do not manage prompts.

questions.reference: [Construct and store reusable prompts with Prompt management in Amazon Bedrock - Amazon BedrockConstruct and store reusable prompts with Prompt management in Amazon Bedrock - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/prompt-management.html)

questions.stem: A chatbot should answer from an enterprise corpus stored in S3 and vectorized for semantic retrieval. Which Bedrock component is most directly involved?

options.explanation: Knowledge Bases provide managed retrieval over enterprise documents using embeddings/vector search patterns.

questions.reference: [Retrieve data and generate AI responses with Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases - Amazon BedrockRetrieve data and generate AI responses with Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/knowledge-base.html)

questions.stem: A developer is debugging why an agent failed to complete a task that required an external API call. Which Bedrock concept should be inspected first?

options.text: The agent's action group definitions and permissions
options.explanation: Agent failures on tool use often come from action schemas or authorization issues. The other options are unrelated.

options.text: The CSS theme of the frontend
The S3 bucket's static website setting

questions.reference: [Add an action group to your agent in Amazon Bedrock - Amazon BedrockAdd an action group to your agent in Amazon Bedrock - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/agents-action-add.html)

questions.stem: A company needs to block certain unsafe topics and redact sensitive content from generated responses across applications. Which Bedrock capability is designed for this?

options.explanation: Guardrails enforce safety policies such as denied topics and sensitive content controls. The other features are not generative-AI safety controls.

questions.reference: [Detect and filter harmful content by using Amazon Bedrock Guardrails - Amazon BedrockDetect and filter harmful content by using Amazon Bedrock Guardrails - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/guardrails.html)

questions.stem: Why should an engineer apply least-privilege IAM to generative AI applications built on Bedrock?

options.text: To restrict access to models and related resources to only what the workload requires
options.explanation: Least-privilege IAM is a core security practice for controlling access. It does not affect context length or override guardrails.

options.text: To increase model context window size

questions.reference: [How Amazon Bedrock works with IAM - Amazon BedrockHow Amazon Bedrock works with IAM - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/security_iam_service-with-iam.html)

questions.stem: A team uses a knowledge base over internal HR documents. What governance control is still important even if retrieval is managed by Bedrock?

options.text: Source data access and document lifecycle controls
options.explanation: Managed retrieval does not remove the need to govern the underlying source documents and their access patterns.

questions.reference: [Retrieve data and generate AI responses with Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases - Amazon BedrockRetrieve data and generate AI responses with Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/knowledge-base.html)

questions.stem: A response contains disallowed content even though the prompt asked the model to avoid it. What is the best platform-level mitigation?

options.text: Apply or strengthen a Bedrock guardrail policy
options.explanation: Guardrails are the platform feature for enforcing safety policies independent of prompt wording alone.

questions.reference: [Detect and filter harmful content by using Amazon Bedrock Guardrails - Amazon BedrockDetect and filter harmful content by using Amazon Bedrock Guardrails - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/guardrails.html)

questions.stem: Which statement about IAM and Bedrock is correct?

options.text: Bedrock integrates with IAM for access control to service actions and resources
options.explanation: Bedrock uses IAM for authorization. The other statements are false.

options.text: IAM is used only for billing reports

questions.reference: [How Amazon Bedrock works with IAM - Amazon BedrockHow Amazon Bedrock works with IAM - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/security_iam_service-with-iam.html)

questions.stem: A team wants to compare candidate models against the same prompts and quality criteria before launch. Which Bedrock capability best supports this?

options.explanation: Model evaluation is used to assess and compare models against defined criteria. The other features do not evaluate generative model quality.

questions.reference: [Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/model-evaluation.html)

questions.stem: Why is prompt versioning important in a production generative AI application?

options.text: It supports repeatability, rollback, and controlled change management
options.explanation: Versioning makes prompt changes auditable and reversible. It does not guarantee perfect model behavior or remove testing.

options.text: It removes the need for testing

questions.reference: [Construct and store reusable prompts with Prompt management in Amazon Bedrock - Amazon BedrockConstruct and store reusable prompts with Prompt management in Amazon Bedrock - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/prompt-management.html)

questions.stem: An evaluation shows a model gives fluent but factually weak answers on company policy questions. What is the best next design change?

options.text: Ground answers with a knowledge base or improve retrieval context
options.explanation: Weak factuality on enterprise questions often points to insufficient grounding. Retrieval or better context is the right next step.

questions.reference: [Retrieve data and generate AI responses with Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases - Amazon BedrockRetrieve data and generate AI responses with Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/knowledge-base.html)

questions.stem: Which practice most directly helps detect regression after a prompt or model change?

options.text: Re-run a representative evaluation set and compare results
options.explanation: Regression detection requires rerunning representative evaluations. Cosmetic changes do not validate model behavior.

questions.reference: [Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/model-evaluation.html)

questions.stem: A team has strict business rules for acceptable outputs. Why is human review still valuable even with automated evaluation?

options.text: Some quality and risk judgments are contextual and not fully captured by simple automated metrics
options.explanation: Human review remains important for nuanced business risk and contextual correctness.

options.text: Automation makes all outputs legally approved
Humans are needed only for IAM setup

questions.reference: [Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/model-evaluation.html)

questions.stem: A prototype chatbot sends every user request to the most expensive model, causing unnecessary spend. What is the best first optimization principle?

options.text: Match model capability and cost to the task instead of always using the largest option
options.explanation: Selecting the right-capability model for each workload is a primary cost-performance optimization.

questions.reference: [Model support by feature in Amazon Bedrock - Amazon BedrockModel support by feature in Amazon Bedrock - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/models-features.html)

questions.stem: An application experiences unpredictable latency because traffic spikes exceed the current inference path. Which Bedrock feature should the engineer review?

options.text: Inference profiles and model routing choices
options.explanation: Inference configuration and routing choices are directly relevant to inference latency behavior.

questions.reference: [Set up a model invocation resource using inference profiles - Amazon BedrockSet up a model invocation resource using inference profiles - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/inference-profiles.html)

questions.stem: What is the operational value of sending Bedrock metrics and logs to CloudWatch?

options.text: It enables visibility into usage, errors, and performance trends
options.explanation: CloudWatch provides observability for usage, errors, and latency. It does not rewrite prompts or replace security controls.

questions.reference: [Monitor the bedrock-runtime endpoint - Amazon BedrockMonitor the bedrock-runtime endpoint - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/monitoring.html)

questions.stem: A team wants to identify which prompts are causing timeouts or retries. Which approach is best?

options.text: Correlate application telemetry with Bedrock monitoring in CloudWatch
options.explanation: Correlated observability helps trace failures to specific workloads and prompts. The other actions do not diagnose latency.

questions.reference: [Monitor the bedrock-runtime endpoint - Amazon BedrockMonitor the bedrock-runtime endpoint - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/monitoring.html)

questions.stem: Why is it useful to benchmark latency and output quality together when evaluating models?

options.text: The cheapest or fastest model may not satisfy the application's quality requirements
options.explanation: Model selection is a tradeoff across quality, latency, and cost, so measuring both is important.

options.text: Latency and quality are unrelated in production

questions.reference: [Model support by feature in Amazon Bedrock - Amazon BedrockModel support by feature in Amazon Bedrock - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/models-features.html)

questions.stem: A team needs image understanding as part of a support workflow. What must be validated before model selection?

options.text: That the chosen Bedrock model supports the required modality and features
options.explanation: Modality and supported feature checks are fundamental during model selection.

options.text: That the model name is shortest
That the S3 bucket has website hosting
That the VPC has exactly two subnets

questions.reference: [Model support by feature in Amazon Bedrock - Amazon BedrockModel support by feature in Amazon Bedrock - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/models-features.html)

questions.stem: A developer wants agent responses to use enterprise search results plus external actions. Which combination is strongest?

options.text: Knowledge bases plus agent action groups
options.explanation: Knowledge bases provide retrieval grounding and action groups let the agent perform tasks, together supporting richer workflows.

questions.reference: [Automate tasks in your application using AI agents - Amazon BedrockAutomate tasks in your application using AI agents - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/agents.html)

questions.stem: A company must prevent PII exposure in generated outputs. Which Bedrock control should be part of the design?

options.text: Guardrails configured for sensitive information policies
options.explanation: Guardrails include protections relevant to sensitive information handling in generated content.

questions.reference: [Detect and filter harmful content by using Amazon Bedrock Guardrails - Amazon BedrockDetect and filter harmful content by using Amazon Bedrock Guardrails - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/guardrails.html)

questions.stem: A prompt update improved helpfulness but increased policy violations. What is the best release decision?

options.text: Do not promote until the safety tradeoff is addressed and re-evaluated
options.explanation: Release decisions should weigh multiple criteria, including policy adherence, not just helpfulness.

options.text: Promote because helpfulness is the only metric that matters

questions.reference: [Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/model-evaluation.html)

questions.stem: A knowledge-base answer quality is poor because relevant passages are not retrieved. Which area should the engineer examine first?

options.text: Data source quality, chunking, and retrieval configuration
options.explanation: Poor retrieval quality often traces to source preparation and retrieval setup, not frontend browser settings.

questions.reference: [Prerequisites for your Amazon Bedrock knowledge base data - Amazon BedrockPrerequisites for your Amazon Bedrock knowledge base data - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/knowledge-base-ds.html)

questions.stem: Why is retrieval-augmented generation often preferred for rapidly changing enterprise policies?

options.text: It can ground responses in current documents rather than relying only on pretrained model knowledge
options.explanation: RAG helps use up-to-date source content, which is useful when policies change frequently.

options.text: It removes the need for permissions

questions.reference: [Retrieve data and generate AI responses with Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases - Amazon BedrockRetrieve data and generate AI responses with Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/knowledge-base.html)

questions.stem: A team wants reusable prompts with approval before production rollout. Which Bedrock feature aligns best?

options.explanation: Managed prompts support reuse and governance better than ad hoc console-only text.

questions.reference: [Construct and store reusable prompts with Prompt management in Amazon Bedrock - Amazon BedrockConstruct and store reusable prompts with Prompt management in Amazon Bedrock - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/prompt-management.html)

questions.stem: An engineer says prompt instructions alone are enough to enforce safety. What is the best response?

options.text: Prompt instructions help, but policy enforcement should also use Bedrock guardrails and governance controls
options.explanation: Prompting is helpful but not sufficient as the only safety mechanism in enterprise workloads.

options.text: That is always correct for regulated workloads

questions.reference: [Detect and filter harmful content by using Amazon Bedrock Guardrails - Amazon BedrockDetect and filter harmful content by using Amazon Bedrock Guardrails - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/guardrails.html)

questions.stem: A model performs well in a lab test but fails when users ask ambiguous follow-up questions. Which evaluation gap is most likely?

options.text: The test set underrepresented realistic multi-turn scenarios
options.explanation: If production issues appear in follow-up turns, the evaluation likely lacked representative conversation scenarios.

options.text: The S3 bucket name was too long

questions.reference: [Using the Converse API (moved) - Amazon BedrockUsing the Converse API (moved) - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/conversation-inference-call.html)

questions.stem: What is the best reason to instrument application-level latency in addition to Bedrock service metrics?

options.text: End-user experience also depends on retrieval, orchestration, and downstream integrations
options.explanation: Total experience includes more than model inference, so application telemetry is essential.

options.text: Bedrock metrics are only for billing
Latency matters only for batch jobs

questions.reference: [Monitor the bedrock-runtime endpoint - Amazon BedrockMonitor the bedrock-runtime endpoint - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/monitoring.html)

questions.stem: A workload requires domain-adapted behavior beyond prompt instructions alone. Which Bedrock capability might be evaluated if supported for the chosen model?

options.text: Custom model or model customization options
options.explanation: Customization options may help when prompt-only steering is insufficient, subject to model support and workload needs.

questions.reference: [Customize your model to improve its performance for your use case - Amazon BedrockCustomize your model to improve its performance for your use case - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/custom-models.html)

questions.stem: A developer is deciding between calling a model directly or through an agent. Which criterion most strongly favors an agent?

options.text: The workflow needs tool use and task orchestration beyond simple text generation
options.explanation: Agents are especially useful when the solution must orchestrate actions and tools rather than only return generated text.

options.text: The response must be in plain text
The documents are stored in S3

questions.reference: [Automate tasks in your application using AI agents - Amazon BedrockAutomate tasks in your application using AI agents - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/agents.html)

questions.stem: Which design choice reduces the blast radius if a compromised component tries to call unauthorized Bedrock actions?

options.text: Use narrowly scoped IAM permissions for each component
options.explanation: Least-privilege IAM limits what a compromised component can do. Broad shared admin roles increase risk.

options.text: Share one admin role across every service

questions.reference: [How Amazon Bedrock works with IAM - Amazon BedrockHow Amazon Bedrock works with IAM - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/security_iam_service-with-iam.html)

questions.stem: A team compares two models and one produces slightly slower responses but materially fewer policy violations. What should happen next?

options.text: Evaluate the tradeoff against business requirements instead of choosing on latency alone
options.explanation: Selection should consider quality, safety, latency, and cost together based on the workload's requirements.

questions.reference: [Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/model-evaluation.html)

questions.stem: A generative AI service seems healthy in Bedrock metrics, but users still report slow answers. What is the best interpretation?

options.text: The bottleneck may be in retrieval, orchestration, or downstream systems rather than model inference itself
options.explanation: Healthy model metrics do not rule out latency elsewhere in the end-to-end application path.

options.text: Knowledge bases cannot be used in production

questions.reference: [Monitor the bedrock-runtime endpoint - Amazon BedrockMonitor the bedrock-runtime endpoint - Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/monitoring.html)
